result.description: Tool's result. Shape entirely determined by the tool. Per MCP convention, structured results are objects; text-only results are strings.

isError.description: MCP-protocol-level `isError` flag from the server (`tools/call` result). `true` means the tool ran but reported a domain error (e.g., 'file not found') — this is NOT a transport error and the node still returns `status: success`. Workflow authors branch on `isError` to handle tool-side failures.

untrustedContent.description: Whether the host marked this result as untrusted-origin content per SECURITY/threat-model-prompt-injection.md (`prompt-injection-mcp-marker`). When `true`, downstream LLM nodes MUST wrap the result in untrusted markers before passing to the model.

markedContent.description: Convenience field present iff (a) the host's MCP client flagged the response as untrusted OR (b) the run is operating under `ctx.trustBoundary === 'untrusted'` (every inbound MCP-server-mount tools/call). The string is the tool's result wrapped in `<UNTRUSTED tool="<name>">...</UNTRUSTED>` per the `prompt-injection-mcp-marker` invariant. Downstream chat-completion nodes can pipe this directly into a user message.
